body:
Keeping your garage door tracks from sticking is mostly about **cleaning them properly and lubricating the right parts—not the tracks themselves**. Here’s a simple, reliable maintenance routine:

* * *

## 1\. Clean the tracks regularly (most important step)

Dirt, dust, and old grease are the main causes of sticking.

  * Close the door and unplug the opener for safety.

  * Use a dry cloth or soft brush to wipe inside both vertical tracks.

  * For stuck-on grime, use mild soap and water or a light degreaser on a rag.

  * Dry everything thoroughly afterward.

👉 The key is keeping the tracks **smooth and debris-free** , not lubricated.

* * *

## 2\. DO NOT grease the tracks

It’s a common mistake, but lubricating the inside of the tracks actually makes sticking worse.

  * Lubricant collects dust and turns into sticky sludge

  * That buildup causes rollers to drag or bind

  * It can even make the door jump or strain the opener [Should You Lubricate a Garage Door Track? - Engineer Fix](https://engineerfix.com/should-you-lubricate-a-garage-door-track/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

Instead, the tracks should stay **clean and dry**.

* * *

## 3\. Lubricate the correct moving parts

Use a **silicone spray or white lithium garage-door lubricant** , and apply it sparingly to:

  * Roller bearings (not the wheel surface)

  * Hinges (pivot points)

  * Springs

  * Bearing plates

After applying, open and close the door a few times to distribute it evenly. [Garage Door Tracks and Rollers - Pro Maintenance Tips](https://keengaragedoors.com/tips-for-maintaining-your-garage-door-tracks-and-rollers/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

* * *

## 4\. Check for mechanical issues that mimic “sticking”

Sometimes it’s not dirt—it’s hardware wear:

  * Bent or misaligned tracks

  * Worn or cracked rollers

  * Loose brackets or bolts

  * Door imbalance (springs weakening)

If the door feels heavy or jerky even after cleaning, this is often the cause.

* * *

## 5\. Tighten and inspect hardware

Once or twice a year:

  * Tighten roller brackets and bolts

  * Look for gaps between rollers and track

  * Check for rubbing points

Loose hardware is a major cause of binding and scraping.

* * *

## 6\. Keep a maintenance schedule

  * Light cleaning: monthly or as needed

  * Full lubrication (moving parts only): every 6–12 months

  * Full inspection: once a year

* * *

### Quick summary

  * ✔ Clean tracks → prevents sticking

  * ❌ Don’t lubricate tracks

  * ✔ Lubricate rollers, hinges, springs

  * ✔ Check alignment and hardware

* * *

If you want, I can walk you through diagnosing a specific sticking spot (like “only on the curve” or “only when closing”), which usually points to a very specific fix.
